content.problemStatement:
You are given an array AAA with length NNN. On each day, the following process was performed:


a new array RRR is created; this array contains all elements AiA_iAi​ such that i=1i=1i=1, i=Ni=Ni=N or min(Ai−1,Ai+1)≤Ai\mathop{\rm min}(A_{i-1}, A_{i+1}) \le A_imin(Ai−1​,Ai+1​)≤Ai​ (2≤i<N2 \le i \lt N2≤i<N), in the original order
AAA is replaced by RRR and NNN is replaced by the length of RRR
In other words, all elements of AAA that are between two bigger elements disappear.

For each element of the original array, calculate the number of the day on which it disappeared (the process starts with day 111), or determine that it never disappeared.
constraints: The sum of N in all test cases does not exceed 10^5
content.inputFormat: The first line of the input contains a single integer T denoting the number of test cases. The description of T test cases follows. The first line of each test case contains a single integer N. The second line contains N space-separated integers A1, A2, …, AN.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing N space-separated integers. For each valid i, the i-th of these integers should be the number of the day on which the i-th element of the original array disappeared, or 0 if it never disappeared.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The array is processed as follows: [3, 2, 5, 4, 1, 7] -> [3, 5, 7] -> [3, 5, 7]. The elements at indices 1, 3, and 4 are removed on days 1, 2, and 1, respectively.
The array is processed as follows: [1, 2, 3, 4, 5] -> [1, 3, 5] -> [1, 5] -> [1, 5]. The elements at indices 1, 2, and 3 are removed on days 1, 2, and 3, respectively.
The array is processed as follows: [4, 3, 2, 1] -> [4, 2] -> [4]. The elements at indices 1, 2, and 3 are removed on days 1, 2, and 3, respectively.

solution.approach: To solve this problem, we will simulate the process described in the problem statement. We will start with the original array and repeatedly apply the process until no more elements can be removed. We will keep track of the number of days each element has been present in the array and output this information at the end.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * N^2) — the outer loop runs T times, and for each test case, we potentially iterate over the array N times, with each iteration taking O(N) time in the worst case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) — we need to store the original array and the current state of the array, which can contain up to N elements.

code.Python3:
import sys
T = int(input())
for _ in range(T):
    N = int(input())
    A = list(map(int, input().split()))
    days = [0] * N
    temp = A[:]
    tempSize = N
    day = 1
    while True:
        newTempSize = 0
        for i in range(tempSize):
            if i == 0 or i == tempSize - 1:
                temp[newTempSize] = A[i]
                newTempSize += 1
            elif A[i] >= A[i - 1] and A[i] >= A[i + 1]:
                temp[newTempSize] = A[i]
                newTempSize += 1
            else:
                days[i] = day
        if newTempSize == tempSize:
            break
        tempSize = newTempSize
        day += 1
    print(' '.join(map(str, days)))
